prayer.title: The Sanctus
prayer.latin_full: Sanctus, Sanctus, Sanctus, Dominus Deus Sabaoth, pleni sunt caeli et terra gloria Tua. Hosanna in excelsis. Benedictus qui venit in nomine Domini. Hosanna in excelsis.
prayer.english_full: Holy, Holy, Holy, Lord God of Hosts, heaven and earth are full of Thy glory. Hosanna in the highest. Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord. Hosanna in the highest.
prayer.new_portion_latin: Sanctus, Sanctus, Sanctus, Dominus Deus Sabaoth.
prayer.new_portion_english: Holy, Holy, Holy, Lord God of Hosts.
prayer.note: Each week, learn the bold portion of the prayer and add it to what you know.

copywork.instructions: Copy and translate. Identify the nouns in each sentence. Which are masculine? Which are feminine? (Hint: -us endings are usually masculine; -a endings are usually feminine.)

prayer.title: The Our Father (Lord's Prayer)
prayer.latin_full: Pater Noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen Tuum. Adveniat regnum Tuum. Fiat voluntas Tua sicut in caelo et in terra.

sentences: Nox est et stellae et lūna sunt clārae.
Mox māter et fīlius domum eunt. Properant quod nox est.

sentences: In hortō sunt multī et pulchrī flōrēs.
Sunt rosae albae et līlia alba quoque.
Alta sunt līlia et pulchrae sunt rosae.
copywork.instructions: Copy and translate. Rōsa and Lilium (from līlium) are still used today as scientific genus names. Find them! Notice alta, albae, pulchrae — these adjectives change ending to match the noun they describe. Are their nouns masculine or feminine?

sentences: Interdum sum dēfessus et patrem et mātrem vidēre cupiō.
Bonus mīles pugnat. Mīles malus domum it.
copywork.instructions: Copy and translate. Find every verb. Which end in -ō (I ___)? Which end in -t (he/she ___)?  The verb cupiō means 'I want' — what does the speaker want to do? Could you use possum here? Try: 'Patrem vidēre nōn possum.'

lessons.title: Future Tense
lessons.focus: Future tense endings added to verbs to show something will happen

practical_latin.english: I am a citizen of Rome

derivatives.gloss: to name a person for a position (v.)
a word that names a person, place, or thing (n.)

prayer.title: The Our Father (Lord's Prayer)
prayer.latin_full: Pater Noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen Tuum. Adveniat regnum Tuum. Fiat voluntas Tua sicut in caelo et in terra.
prayer.english_full: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven.

prayer.new_portion_english: on earth as it is in heaven.

sentences: Hī quattuor puerī ā casīs ad silvam properant.
Puerī domum eunt ubi cēna in mēnsā parāta est.
copywork.instructions: Copy and translate. These verbs are present tense. To make them future, add -bō or -bit. Try changing properant → properābunt (they will hurry) and eunt → ībunt (they will go). Find the numbers in the first sentence (quattuor = how many?).

sentences: Māne in carrō pater et fīlia per agrōs et silvam properant.
Subitō in agrō quattuor animālia videntur.
Vesperī pater et fīlia in magnō oppidō sunt.
Iūlia Annam exspectat et prope fenestram stat.
copywork.instructions: Copy and translate. Find: (1) a form of esse, (2) two adjectives, (3) two adverbs (māne, vesperī, subitō, prope), (4) two prepositions. Circle any number words. Bonus: what constellation shares a name with a word in this passage?

lessons.title: Other Words
lessons.focus: Conjunctions and particles — small words that connect or modify ideas

prayer.title: The Our Father (Lord's Prayer)
prayer.latin_full: Pater Noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen Tuum. Adveniat regnum Tuum. Fiat voluntas Tua sicut in caelo et in terra. Panem nostrum cotidianum da nobis hodie, et dimitte nobis debita nostra, sicut et nos dimittimus debitoribus nostris. Et ne nos inducas in tentationem, sed libera nos a malo. Amen.
prayer.english_full: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread, and forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors. And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. Amen.
prayer.new_portion_latin: Panem nostrum cotidianum da nobis hodie,
prayer.new_portion_english: Give us this day our daily bread,
prayer.note: Each week, learn the bold portion of the prayer and add it to what you know.

sentences: Patria nostra in bellō nōn pugnat, sed fīnitimī nostrī pugnant et nōs sumus sociī.
Cum fīnitimī auxilium rogant, necesse est īre.
Multī numquam iterum venient. Multī vītās dabunt.
copywork.instructions: Copy and translate. Underline every conjunction (sed, et, cum, neque, quod, quamquam, sicut). What two things or ideas does each conjunction connect or contrast? Find the word meaning 'never' — numquam.

sentences: Ille vir oculōs habet, sed nūllās rēs videt.
Ubi cum fīliō ambulat, tūtus est.
Omnēs virī et puerī illī virō auxilium dare dēbent.
Vir erat mīles et prō patriā pugnābat.
copywork.instructions: Copy and translate. Find the 1st declension nouns (base form ends in -a). Try to identify each noun's case: nominative (subject), accusative (direct object), or dative (indirect object, often meaning 'to/for'). How do the endings help you?

lessons.title: Declensions
lessons.focus: First Declension plural endings; completing the full 1st declension paradigm

prayer.title: The Our Father (Lord's Prayer)
prayer.latin_full: Pater Noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen Tuum. Adveniat regnum Tuum. Fiat voluntas Tua sicut in caelo et in terra. Panem nostrum cotidianum da nobis hodie, et dimitte nobis debita nostra, sicut et nos dimittimus debitoribus nostris. Et ne nos inducas in tentationem, sed libera nos a malo. Amen.
prayer.english_full: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread, and forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors. And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. Amen.
prayer.new_portion_latin: Et ne nos inducas in tentationem,
prayer.new_portion_english: And lead us not into temptation,
prayer.note: Each week, learn the bold portion of the prayer and add it to what you know.

paradigms.label: First Declension Plural Endings
paradigms.note: ae has the long A sound

sentences: Circum castra nostra est mūrus altus.
Inter castra hostium et castra nostra illud flūmen fluit.
copywork.instructions: Copy and translate. Find all plural nouns — what are their endings? Find any 1st declension nouns and identify their case. Find four different prepositions (trāns, circum, inter, in) — what does each one mean?

lessons.title: First Conjugation
lessons.focus: Conjugations are verb groups; 1st conjugation verbs are conjugated by adding pronoun endings to the stem

derivatives.gloss: kind and friendly like an uncle (adj.)

prayer.title: The Our Father (Lord's Prayer)
prayer.latin_full: Pater Noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen Tuum. Adveniat regnum Tuum. Fiat voluntas Tua sicut in caelo et in terra. Panem nostrum cotidianum da nobis hodie, et dimitte nobis debita nostra, sicut et nos dimittimus debitoribus nostris. Et ne nos inducas in tentationem, sed libera nos a malo. Amen.
prayer.english_full: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread, and forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors. And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. Amen.
prayer.new_portion_latin: sed libera nos a malo. Amen.
prayer.new_portion_english: but deliver us from evil. Amen.
prayer.note: Final line — the full Pater Noster is now complete.

paradigms.label: First Conjugation — amo (to love), Present Tense

sentences: Sine illō auxiliō avunculus hortum nōn bene cūrat.
Hiems bene armāta flōrēs flūminaque oppugnat et semper superat.
Aestās, tamen, est mihi grātior quam hiems.
copywork.instructions: Copy and translate. Underline all 1st conjugation verbs (built on -āre stems: iuvat, cūrat, oppugnat, superat). What person and number is each? Give the infinitive: iuvāre, cūrāre, etc. Find a comparative adjective (hint: -ior ending means 'more ___').

sentences: Sī hodiē oppugnābunt, tamen fortiter et ācriter pugnābimus.
copywork.instructions: Copy and translate. This is your full grammar review! Find: (1) a conjunction, (2) a 1st declension noun, (3) a 1st conjugation verb, (4) a form of esse, (5) a question word (if any), (6) a future tense verb (-bō/-bit/-bunt ending). Bonus: which verbs are future passive?

sanctus.title: The Sanctus
sanctus.latin: Sanctus, Sanctus, Sanctus, Dominus Deus Sabaoth, pleni sunt caeli et terra gloria Tua. Hosanna in excelsis. Benedictus qui venit in nomine Domini. Hosanna in excelsis.
sanctus.english: Holy, Holy, Holy, Lord God of Hosts, heaven and earth are full of Thy glory. Hosanna in the highest. Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord. Hosanna in the highest.

gloria-patri.title: Gloria Patri
gloria-patri.latin: Gloria Patri et Filio et Spiritui Sancto, sicut erat in principio et nunc et semper et in saecula saeculorum. Amen.
gloria-patri.english: Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen.

table-blessing.title: The Table Blessing
table-blessing.latin: Benedic, Domine, nos et haec Tua dona, quae de Tua largitate sumus sumpturi, per Christum Dominum nostrum. Amen.
table-blessing.english: Bless us, O Lord, and these Thy gifts, which from Thy bounty we are about to receive, through Christ our Lord. Amen.

pater-noster.title: The Our Father (Lord's Prayer)
pater-noster.latin: Pater Noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen Tuum. Adveniat regnum Tuum. Fiat voluntas Tua sicut in caelo et in terra. Panem nostrum cotidianum da nobis hodie, et dimitte nobis debita nostra, sicut et nos dimittimus debitoribus nostris. Et ne nos inducas in tentationem, sed libera nos a malo. Amen.
pater-noster.english: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread, and forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors. And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. Amen.
pater-noster.note: Second half learned across L21–25; full prayer complete at L25
